real_insight: Most 'our authentication' / 'our identity' / 'our payments' framings name inherited substrates, not inventions. The customer-need outlasts your tenure by design. Audit your team's ownership language for claims that don't hold.
objection: I expected this to be 'humility theater.' Instead the analog is concrete, with named historical references: Roman tesserae for guild-membership verification (second century), Diffie-Hellman 1976, OAuth 2.0 RFC 6749 (2012, three years before the SaaS company existed), Postgres SCRAM-SHA-256 since v10, Auth0, AWS Cognito. The point about scoping (when the system is named correctly as inherited substrate, design decisions widen) is operationally correct — I have watched bad scoping decisions specifically because a founding engineer treated inherited substrate as proprietary. The verse's coupling of 'audit your ownership language' to actual design consequences (depending on Auth0 / OAuth library instead of rolling custom JWT) makes it falsifiable. Cannot dismiss as wisdom-theater.
